title: SSRF to Cloud Metadata Endpoint (169.254.169.254)
description:
Detects outbound network connections to the cloud instance metadata service
(169.254.169.254) from web application or scripting processes. This indicates
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) where an attacker exploits a web app to
access the OCI/AWS/GCP/Azure IMDS endpoint and steal credentials, instance
identity tokens, or configuration data.
Covers MITRE ATT&CK T1552.005 (Cloud Instance Metadata API).
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and (('Destination IP' = '169.254.169.254' and Initiated = 'true') and (('Process Name' like '*\\python.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\pythonw.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\python3.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\node.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\java.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\javaw.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\php.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\ruby.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\dotnet.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\httpd.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\nginx.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\w3wp.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\curl.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\wget.exe')))
falsepositives: Legitimate cloud SDK calls from web applications (rare — most use SDKs, not direct IMDS)
